root_text.an3.74:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā ānando vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.an3.74:1.2: Atha kho abhayo ca licchavi paṇḍitakumārako ca licchavi yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho abhayo licchavi āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.74:1.3: “nigaṇṭho, bhante, nāṭaputto sabbaññū sabbadassāvī aparisesaṁ ñāṇadassanaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an3.74:1.4: ‘carato ca me tiṭṭhato ca suttassa ca jāgarassa ca satataṁ samitaṁ ñāṇadassanaṁ paccupaṭṭhitan’ti.
root_text.an3.74:1.5: So purāṇānaṁ kammānaṁ tapasā byantībhāvaṁ paññāpeti navānaṁ kammānaṁ akaraṇā setughātaṁ.
root_text.an3.74:1.6: Iti kammakkhayā dukkhakkhayo, dukkhakkhayā vedanākkhayo, vedanākkhayā sabbaṁ dukkhaṁ nijjiṇṇaṁ bhavissati—
root_text.an3.74:1.7: evametissā sandiṭṭhikāya nijjarāya visuddhiyā samatikkamo hoti.
root_text.an3.74:2.1: “Tisso kho imā, abhaya, nijjarā visuddhiyo tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena sammadakkhātā sattānaṁ visuddhiyā sokaparidevānaṁ samatikkamāya dukkhadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamāya ñāyassa adhigamāya nibbānassa sacchikiriyāya.
root_text.an3.74:2.3: Idha, abhaya, bhikkhu sīlavā hoti …pe… samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu.
root_text.an3.74:2.4: So navañca kammaṁ na karoti, purāṇañca kammaṁ phussa phussa byantīkaroti.
root_text.an3.74:2.5: Sandiṭṭhikā nijjarā akālikā ehipassikā opaneyyikā paccattaṁ veditabbā viññūhīti.
root_text.an3.74:3.1: Sa kho so, abhaya, bhikkhu evaṁ sīlasampanno vivicceva kāmehi …pe… catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an3.74:3.2: So navañca kammaṁ na karoti, purāṇañca kammaṁ phussa phussa byantīkaroti.
root_text.an3.74:3.3: Sandiṭṭhikā nijjarā akālikā ehipassikā opaneyyikā paccattaṁ veditabbā viññūhīti.
root_text.an3.74:4.1: Sa kho so, abhaya, bhikkhu evaṁ samādhisampanno āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an3.74:4.2: So navañca kammaṁ na karoti, purāṇañca kammaṁ phussa phussa byantīkaroti.
root_text.an3.74:4.3: Sandiṭṭhikā nijjarā akālikā ehipassikā opaneyyikā paccattaṁ veditabbā viññūhīti.
root_text.an3.74:4.4: Imā kho, abhaya, tisso nijjarā visuddhiyo tena bhagavatā jānatā passatā arahatā sammāsambuddhena sammadakkhātā sattānaṁ visuddhiyā sokaparidevānaṁ samatikkamāya dukkhadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamāya ñāyassa adhigamāya nibbānassa sacchikiriyāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.74:5.1: Evaṁ vutte, paṇḍitakumārako licchavi abhayaṁ licchaviṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.74:5.2: “kiṁ pana tvaṁ, samma abhaya, āyasmato ānandassa subhāsitaṁ subhāsitato nābbhanumodasī”ti?
root_text.an3.74:5.3: “Kyāhaṁ, samma paṇḍitakumāraka, āyasmato ānandassa subhāsitaṁ subhāsitato nābbhanumodissāmi.
root_text.an3.74:5.4: Muddhāpi tassa vipateyya yo āyasmato ānandassa subhāsitaṁ subhāsitato nābbhanumodeyyā”ti.

translation_text.an3.74:1.1: At one time Venerable Ānanda was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.an3.74:1.2: Then the Licchavis Abhaya and Paṇḍitakumāra went up to Venerable Ānanda, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an3.74:1.3: “Sir, the Jain ascetic of the Ñātika clan claims to be all-knowing and all-seeing, to know and see everything without exception, thus:
translation_text.an3.74:1.4: ‘Knowledge and vision are constantly and continually present to me, while walking, standing, sleeping, and waking.’
translation_text.an3.74:1.5: He advocates the elimination of past deeds by fervent mortification, and breaking the bridge by not doing any new deeds.
translation_text.an3.74:1.6: So with the ending of deeds, suffering ends; with the ending of suffering, feeling ends; and with the ending of feeling, all suffering will have been worn away.
translation_text.an3.74:1.7: This is how to go beyond suffering by means of this purification by wearing away in the present life.
translation_text.an3.74:1.8: What, sir, does the Buddha say about this?”
translation_text.an3.74:2.1: “Abhaya, these three kinds of purification by wearing away have been rightly explained by the Blessed One, who knows and sees, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha. They are in order to purify sentient beings, to get past sorrow and crying, to make an end of pain and sadness, to discover the system, and to realize extinguishment.
translation_text.an3.74:2.3: It’s when, Abhaya, a mendicant is ethical, restrained in the monastic code, conducting themselves well and resorting for alms in suitable places. Seeing danger in the slightest fault, they keep the rules they’ve undertaken.
translation_text.an3.74:2.4: They don’t perform any new deeds, and old deeds are eliminated by experiencing their results little by little.
translation_text.an3.74:2.5: This wearing away is apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.
translation_text.an3.74:3.1: Then a mendicant accomplished in ethics, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, enters and remains in the first absorption … second absorption … third absorption … fourth absorption.
translation_text.an3.74:3.2: They don’t perform any new deeds, and old deeds are eliminated by experiencing their results little by little.
translation_text.an3.74:3.3: This wearing away is apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.
translation_text.an3.74:4.1: Then a mendicant accomplished in immersion realizes the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. And they live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an3.74:4.2: They don’t perform any new deeds, and old deeds are eliminated by experiencing their results little by little.
translation_text.an3.74:4.3: This wearing away is apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.
translation_text.an3.74:4.4: These are the three kinds of purification by wearing away that have been rightly explained by the Buddha … in order to realize extinguishment.”
translation_text.an3.74:5.1: When he said this, Paṇḍitakumāra said to Abhaya,
translation_text.an3.74:5.2: “Dear Abhaya, is there anything in what Ānanda has said so well that you would disagree with?”
translation_text.an3.74:5.3: “How could I not agree with what was said so well by Ānanda?
translation_text.an3.74:5.4: If anyone didn’t agree with him, their head would explode!”

variant_text.an3.74:4.1: evaṁ samādhisampanno → evaṁ sīlasampanno evaṁ samādhisampanno (bj, sya-all, km); evaṁ sīlasampanno …pe… (pts1ed)

reference_text.an3.74:1.1: csp1ed15.222, csp2ed15.222, ms15A3_678, msdiv75, pts-vp-pli1.221, sya20.284
